accurate.why: All claims (PL 119-5, H.J.Res.25, IRS rule title, Federal Register citation, chapter 8 of title 5) match the bill text and verified record.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or false claims; IRS attribution and CRA context are accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as nullifying the specified IRS digital asset broker reporting rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes the disapproval of the IRS rule under chapter 8 of title 5, though it omits the underlying section 6045/IIJA context.

exceptions_conditions.why: This one-provision disapproval resolution contains no material exceptions or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: The resolution specifies no effective dates or deadlines beyond the rule's Federal Register publication date, which is included.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect—disapproval rendering the rule of no force or effect—though the sentence is cut off before fully stating it.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is abruptly truncated mid-sentence ('declares that the'), leaving it incomplete and broken.

concise.why: No padding or filler; tightly written.

correct_entities.why: IRS, the rule title, Federal Register citation, and chapter 8 of title 5 are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The Federal Register page (106928), date (December 30, 2024), and law number are all correct.
